evals.prompt: Build an admin users page in the existing Next.js App Router app with URL-backed filters, paginated GraphQL data, loading/error/empty states, and an edit mutation.
evals.expected_output: A project-aligned feature using existing routing, query, form, cache, UI, accessibility, and responsive patterns.
expectations: Inspects and follows the nearest feature structure before adding abstractions
Keeps server state in the established query layer and URL state shareable
Defines loading, error, empty, success, and mutation feedback states
Updates or invalidates cache through the project's established query keys

evals.prompt: Diagnose a hydration mismatch on a dashboard card that formats the current date and reads localStorage during render. Do not implement a fix.
evals.expected_output: An evidence-based diagnosis separating nondeterministic server rendering from browser-only storage access and explaining safe correction options without editing files.
expectations: Identifies server/client date divergence as a hydration risk
Identifies render-time localStorage access as browser-only behavior
Recommends the smallest compatible boundary or initialization strategy
